description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered, multi-toned shingles, giving it a rustic, traditional appearance. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden siding with visible timber framing accents, especially around the upper story windows. The lower portion of the house is constructed from rough-cut stone, providing a sturdy base and contrasting texture. Windows are symmetrically arranged with wooden frames and divided panes, while the main entryway is a simple wooden door set into the stone facade. Decorative wooden crossbeams and trim add architectural interest to the upper story. The overall design evokes a classic European or Tudor-style cottage, suitable for a quaint urban neighborhood.
description: A rustic, gable-roofed house with timber framing, stone base, and symmetrical multi-pane windows, evoking a traditional European cottage style.
description_view_0: Front view shows the steep gable roof, a centered upper window with timber framing, and a large lower window above the stone base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long, shingled roof, two sets of paired windows, and the stone foundation running the length of the house.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front with a gable roof, centered upper and lower windows, and visible timber framing details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view features the main entry door set in the stone base, paired windows, and the same long, shingled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door set in stone base, no porch or overhang, minimal decorative elements
